displayTitle: The Test Is Won Before the Penetrant

beats.narration: An indication can be lost before the first drop goes on — wrong temperature, wrong light, or a surface already sealed shut.

payload.title: BEFORE THE PENETRANT
payload.sub: Everything around the test decides what it can find.

beats.narration: Temperature first. Hold everything between forty and one-twenty-five Fahrenheit. Cold thickens penetrant; heat dries the film and fades fluorescent dye.
Lighting flips by dye. Visible red wants a hundred footcandles of white. Fluorescent inverts it — a dark booth and a flood of UV-A.
Now the trap. Peening, blasting, sanding, grinding smear metal over crack mouths and blind the test. A controlled etch reopens them.
Hence the sequence rule. Inspect after anything that creates a flaw — before anything that smears, seals, or coats it. Strip, clean, etch, dry, test.

payload.sub: Read the traveler, not the shine.
